title: scleranthus perennis subsp. marginatus
intro: Scleranthus perennis subsp. marginatus, also known as the alpine knot grass, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, making it a popular choice for treating various health issues. The plant has been found to offer several health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of menstrual cramps and menopausal hot flashes. Scleranthus perennis subsp. marginatus can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for a range of applications in traditional medicine.
